title: Sick-Day Indian Dried Fruit Jalebi
description: A charred, fluffy, and comforting Indian dish featuring dried fruit, perfect for a sick day. This recipe is inspired by Goan cuisine and is high-protein friendly.

ingredients.name: Dried Fruit (mixed types)
ingredients.notes: such as cranberries, apricots, and raisins

instructions: Combine dried fruit, yeast, and warm water to create a mixture that foams and bubbles.
Let the mixture ferment for 4-6 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
Add the flour, salt, sugar, and baking soda to the mixture and knead for 10 minutes until smooth.
Cover the dough and let it rise for another 2-3 hours, or until it has doubled in size again.
Heat the ghee or oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, use a piping bag or a plastic bag with a corner cut off to pipe the dough into long, thin strands.
Fry the jalebi until they are golden brown and puffed up, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Drain the jalebi on paper towels and dust with cardamom powder.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves and serve warm.
